title: 10 best herbal creams for gum infection

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Help combat bacterial and fungal infections by killing or inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Calming and Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Soothe and reduce swelling in inflamed gums, making it an effective treatment for gum infections such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
Antioxidant Properties: Neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, preventing further inflammation and promoting the repair of damaged gum tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with gum infection because it contains a compound called melaleuca oil, which has potent antimicrobial properties. This oil works to combat the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as periodontitis and gingivitis, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the gum tissue allows it to target the source of the infection, providing relief from pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with gum infections. Regular use can lead to improved oral health and gum stability.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that cause gum infections.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the gums from oxidative stress and prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oil extracted from Melaleuca alternifolia leaves is commonly used in creams for gum infection due to its antibacterial properties.
Barks: Melaleuca alternifolia bark is used to make creams for gum infection because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Leaves: Another part of Melaleuca alternifolia, the leaves, are also used to make creams for gum infection due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 25g of dried roots in late summer.
Infuse 25g of the dried leaves and roots in 250ml of carrier oil for 2 hours.
Filter the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container after 2 hours.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the filtered mixture and heat gently.
Pour the melted mixture into a mold and allow it to set for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Calendula, the active compound in pot marigold cream, exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacteria and fungi associated with gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum infections, creating a conducive environment for healthy tissue growth and repair.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Calendula has been shown to stimulate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and reducing oxidative stress, making it an effective agent in treating gum infection-related wounds or lesions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, making it an effective treatment for gum infections. Additionally, Calendula officinalis has been traditionally used to promote tissue repair and wound healing, which can aid in the recovery of gum tissue and prevent further infection. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote healthy gums.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the gums, thereby alleviating gum infection.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Flavonoids (such as quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and promote healing of the gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing infection.
Leaves: They contain compounds that help to reduce swelling and fight bacterial growth, promoting a healthy gum environment.
Roots: They are used for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to combat gum infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a dry sunny morning with scissors or pinch them off.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the calendula infusion and combine it with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Blend the calendula infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and pour it into a clean glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the gums, reducing the severity of gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, including gingerol and paradol, can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healing and preventing further damage.

Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial agents, such as gingerol and zingiberene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that cause gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and E. coli.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to combat the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a popular natural remedy for gum health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting a faster recovery.
Shogaols: Shogaols, a type of gingerol, exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help combat the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Escherichia.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy gum environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help combat gum infections.

Buds: Ginger buds are used to make creams that have antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning.
Steam 30 grams of the rhizomes in 500 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter with 100 milliliters of coconut oil.
Add 100 milliliters of the steamed rhizome infusion to the beeswax mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric's antimicrobial compounds, such as curcuminoids, help fight off bacteria and other microorganisms that cause gum infections by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from multiplying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in turmeric cream, including curcumin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Curcumin, the active compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections. The cream's bioavailability allows curcumin to penetrate deep into the gums, promoting healing and reducing swelling. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the gums from further damage, making it an effective treatment for gum infections and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and fungi causing gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, prevent tissue damage, and promote the healing of gum tissues.

Turmerones: Turmerones are sesquiterpenes with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help eliminate bacteria and other pathogens causing gum infections, and reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum infections.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain compounds like curcumin, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds contain volatile oils that have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote healing in gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Grate 100 grams of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in a mortar and pestle to release their active properties.
Combine 100 milliliters of distilled water with 20 grams of grated curcuma longa in the mortar.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until it reduces to half its original volume stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar to remove solids and obtain clear liquid.
Allow the liquid to cool to room temperature then mix it with 200 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal sage creams contain antimicrobial compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Staphylococcus aureus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with gum infections, promoting a faster recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants like carnosic acid, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of tissue damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties. The active compounds present in sage, such as salvene and carnosic acid, exhibit potent antibacterial and antifungal activities, effectively reducing the severity of gum infections. Additionally, sage creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: As a sesquiterpene, it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the gums from infection.
Salvianolic acid A: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections.
Flowers: They possess anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infections.
Roots: They contain compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, helping to prevent the spread of gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried salvia officinalis leaves at their peak potency typically during the early morning hours.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of the dried leaves in 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a pure extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Combine the melted wax mixture with 2 ounces of aloe vera gel and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's active compounds have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with gum infections, allowing for a faster recovery.

Wound-healing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing by improving blood flow, reducing scarring, and promoting tissue repair, making it an effective treatment for gum wounds and abscesses.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause gum infections. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation, promoting a faster recovery and healthier gums. remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a terpene, helps with gum infections by reducing inflammation and combating bacterial growth.
Cineole: Cineole, a terpene, exhibits antimicrobial properties that aid in the control of gum infection-causing bacteria.

Essential oil extracted from Leaves: While leaves are mentioned above, it's worth noting that the essential oil extracted from the leaves is the main active ingredient used in creams for gum infections due to its potent antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 100 grams of beeswax for the recipe.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves to the oil mixture and let it infuse for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture and discard the solids then add 20 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of lanolin.
Whip the mixture until it thickens then pour it into containers and let it cool for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak potency for optimal use.
Steep 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove plant material completely.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create base.
Whip the base mixture with an electric mixer until it reaches desired consistency for application.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the ground material in 4 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and use a dropper to transfer it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans and Escherichia coli, which can cause gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing the affected area.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer at 30-40°c for 24 hours.
Steep 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil at 45-55°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10-15% of the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a 2:1 ratio to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Wild marjoram's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial growth in infected gums, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healing and comfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh origanum vulgare leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 30°c for 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried origanum vulgare leaves with 2 ounces of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves creams helps with gum infection because they contain potent antimicrobial compounds like eugenol, which have been shown to effectively combat bacterial growth that causes periodontal disease. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with infected gums. remedy_recipe: Harvest syzygium aromaticum bark in late spring or early summer when it contains maximum amount of active compounds.
Dry the harvested bark using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or until crumbly texture achieved.
Grind 30 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 1 hour.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy texture achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with gum infection because they contain a unique compound that targets the root cause of the issue. The cream's active ingredient, fatty acid extract, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and prevent further bacterial growth in the affected area. By directly targeting the gum tissue, saw palmetto cream can promote healing, alleviate pain and discomfort, and eliminate bacteria that contribute to infection, ultimately leading to a healthier and stronger set of gums.

Wound healing enhancement: Saw palmetto cream's fatty acids have been shown to enhance wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing scar formation, making it effective in supporting the recovery of gum tissues affected by infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant are rich in fatty acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of infected gums.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out one hundred grams of petroleum jelly and melt it slowly in a double boiler.
Add fifteen milliliters of distilled water to the melted petroleum jelly and stir until fully incorporated.
Combine thirty grams of dried serenoa repens root powder with twenty-five milliliters of glycerin and mix thoroughly.
Gradually add the serenoa repens mixture to the melted petroleum jelly and water mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least two hours before transferring it to air-tight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with gum infection because of its unique properties that combat bacterial growth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Licoricidin: This bioactive compound has been found to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, while also promoting tissue repair and healing.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in licorice creams helps to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, which can exacerbate gum infections and slow down the healing process.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered root with 200 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 150 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture for 30 minutes or until it reaches 60°c.
Remove from heat and stir in 50 grams of vitamin e oil and 20 grams of tea tree essential oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with gum infection because they possess potent antimicrobial properties that can effectively combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, measuring out exactly 2 ounces for the recipe.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves in a still or using a steam distillation kit for 2 hours.
Combine the essential oil extracted from the steaming process with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the mixture of coconut oil, beeswax, and peppermint essential oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted cream base into glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them completely in a warm oven for four hours at 150°f.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Mix the powdered rosemary leaves with 200ml of distilled water and 20g of beeswax to create a smooth paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and forms a creamy consistency.
Strain the hot cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool before applying it to affected gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The herb's essential oils, such as thymol and linalool, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and pain in the affected area. Additionally, marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and ease discomfort, promoting healing and soothing gums. As a result, sweet marjoram creams can provide natural relief from gum infection symptoms, making it an effective and gentle alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, help to combat bacterial infections in the gums, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infection, promoting healing and regeneration of tissues.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Leaves: Containing essential oils that have antibacterial properties, which aid in preventing the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the origanum majorana leaves in a dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours or until crumbly.
Grind 20g of dried origanum majorana leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Melt 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and combine it with the origanum majorana powder stirring well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool at room temperature for 2 hours before refrigeration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial properties. The active compound, anethole, inhibits the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe swollen gums and reduce pain. As a natural antiseptic, it also helps to prevent further infection by creating a protective barrier against harmful bacteria. By targeting both the underlying infection and its symptoms, herbal fennel creams provide effective relief from gum infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Fennel cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel cream reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with gum infections, making it an effective natural remedy for periodontitis and gingivitis.
Antioxidant property: Fennel's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that damage gum tissue, promoting healthy gums and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a natural oil with antimicrobial properties that effectively combat bacteria causing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and roots in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare material using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve quality.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with two parts coconut oil and infuse in a warm dark place for 14 days allowing for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids and filtering resulting liquid through a coffee filter for clarity.
Mix one part of the filtered foeniculum vulgare cream with one part beeswax and melt at 65 degrees celsius in a double boiler whipping until smooth and consistent texture is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill creams helps with gum infection because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptus and peppermint oils, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in affected areas. Additionally, dill's antibacterial compounds help combat bacterial growth, promoting a healthy environment for gums and preventing further infection. As a result, the cream helps alleviate symptoms of gum disease, including redness, bleeding, and bad breath, ultimately supporting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Dill's antimicrobial compounds, such as carvone and limonene, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dill's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, while also preventing further damage to the gums and surrounding tissues.

Antioxidant properties: Dill's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by gum infection, promoting a faster and more effective recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dill roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Rhyzomes: Dill rhyzomes contain antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria causing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried anethum graveolens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered anethum graveolens with 20% petroleum jelly in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Combine the mixture with 5% vitamin e oil and stir until well blended for about 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 50°c to 60°c using a double boiler for approximately 30 minutes.
Strain the hot mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into sterilized glass jars and let it cool.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gum infection are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as inflammation, pain, and redness. These creams contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties from herbs like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and eucalyptus. Examples include creams containing clove oil, chamomile, and myrrh, which have been shown to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing, improving oral health and reducing the risk of gum disease, ultimately enhancing overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal irritated skin. They also combat fungal and bacterial infections, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with gum infection is a blend of aloe vera, tea tree oil, and eucalyptus. Aloe vera soothes and calms the gums, while tea tree oil's antibacterial properties combat infection. Eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease pain. Apply a mixture of equal parts aloe vera and tea tree oil to the affected area, followed by a few drops of eucalyptus oil. This combination promotes healing, reduces inflammation, and alleviates discomfort.
